exam.name: CompTIA A+ Core 1
exam.category_description: Exam-aligned prep for IT certifications with clear, syllabus-mapped coverage across hardware, networking, devices, troubleshooting, and modern workplace technologies.
exam.hero_headline: Build Your IT Support Foundation with CompTIA A+ Core 1.
exam.hero_subheadline: Master mobile devices, networking, hardware, cloud basics, and troubleshooting skills mapped directly to the 220-1201 exam objectives.
exam.companion_problem: CompTIA A+ Core 1 spans a wide range of practical IT support topics, and it is easy to lose track of how hardware, networking, mobile devices, and troubleshooting objectives fit together.
exam.companion_solution: Only Ever turns the official CompTIA A+ Core 1 objectives into focused 15-minute study sessions so you can build durable understanding across the 220-1201 syllabus without drifting off-spec.
exam.learner_profile: Aspiring IT support technician, help desk analyst, desktop support specialist, or career switcher preparing for an entry-level technical role. Needs structured, practical explanations of devices, networking, components, cloud basics, and troubleshooting workflows.

sections.title: Mobile Devices
sections.description: Monitor, replace, and configure mobile device hardware, accessories, and network connectivity.

topics.title: Mobile Device Hardware Components
topics.description: Identify and use appropriate replacement techniques for internal mobile device hardware and security components.
learning_objectives: Monitor and replace laptop batteries, keyboards, RAM, and solid-state drives.
Identify internal wireless cards, Wi-Fi antennas, and camera/microphone components.
Describe the function of physical privacy components, biometrics, and near-field scanners on mobile devices.

topics.title: Mobile Device Connectivity and Accessories
topics.description: Compare and contrast wired and wireless connectivity options and common accessories used with mobile devices.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between USB, Lightning, NFC, and Bluetooth connection methods.
Explain the use of mobile hotspots and tethering.
Compare mobile accessories such as docking stations, port replicators, headsets, and trackpads.

topics.title: Mobile Network Connectivity and MDM
topics.description: Configure basic mobile device network connectivity, synchronization, and Mobile Device Management (MDM) features.
learning_objectives: Enable and configure cellular data, Wi-Fi, eSIMs, and Bluetooth pairing.
Explain the role of location services like GPS and cellular location tracking.
Summarize the features of Mobile Device Management (MDM), corporate configurations, and mobile synchronization options.

sections.title: Networking
sections.description: Understand core networking principles, protocols, hardware, and configuration for SOHO environments.

topics.title: TCP and UDP Ports and Protocols
topics.description: Compare and contrast Transmission Control Protocol (TCP) and User Datagram Protocol (UDP) and recognize common port numbers.
learning_objectives: Identify the differences between TCP (connection-oriented) and UDP (connectionless).
Memorize common port numbers for File Transfer Protocol (FTP), SSH, Telnet, SMTP, and DNS.
Understand the ports and protocols for web traffic (HTTP/HTTPS), email (POP3/IMAP), and remote management (RDP, SMB).

topics.title: Wireless Networking Technologies
topics.description: Explain wireless frequency bands, channels, and other wireless standards such as Bluetooth and RFID.
learning_objectives: Compare 2.4GHz, 5GHz, and 6GHz wireless frequencies and their characteristics.
Explain channel regulations, widths, and selections in 802.11 standards.
Describe the uses and characteristics of Bluetooth, NFC, and RFID technologies.

topics.title: Networked Host Services and Appliances
topics.description: Summarize the functions provided by networked servers, internet appliances, and legacy/embedded systems.
learning_objectives: Identify the roles of common network servers including DNS, DHCP, Print, Mail, and AAA.
Explain the purpose of internet appliances like Spam gateways, UTM devices, Load balancers, and Proxy servers.
Understand the network implications of legacy systems, SCADA, and IoT devices.

topics.title: Network Configuration Concepts
topics.description: Explain fundamental network configuration concepts including DNS records, DHCP scopes, VLANs, and VPNs.
learning_objectives: Identify the differences between DNS records such as A, AAAA, CNAME, and MX.
Explain DNS spam management concepts including TXT, DKIM, SPF, and DMARC.
Describe DHCP configuration terms like leases, reservations, scope, and exclusions, as well as the purpose of VLANs and VPNs.

topics.title: Networking Hardware Devices
topics.description: Compare and contrast common networking hardware including routers, switches, and modems.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between the functions of routers, managed/unmanaged switches, and access points.
Explain the roles of firewalls, patch panels, and Network Interface Cards (NICs) with MAC addresses.
Describe Power over Ethernet (PoE) standard hardware and various modems/ONTs.

topics.title: SOHO Network Configuration
topics.description: Configure basic wired and wireless Small Office/Home Office (SOHO) networks.
learning_objectives: Determine appropriate IP addressing utilizing IPv4 and IPv6.
Configure static and dynamic IP addressing parameters including subnet masks and gateways.
Explain the circumstances that lead to an Automatic Private IP Addressing (APIPA) assignment.

topics.title: Internet Connection and Network Types
topics.description: Compare and contrast different Internet connection options and organizational network architectures.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between Internet connection types such as satellite, fiber, cable, DSL, and cellular.
Define the scope of different network types including LAN, WAN, PAN, MAN, SAN, and WLAN.
Evaluate the characteristics and appropriate use cases for each network architecture.

topics.title: Networking Tools
topics.description: Explain the purpose of various networking tools used for cable installation and troubleshooting.
learning_objectives: Identify the uses of hardware tools such as crimpers, cable strippers, and punchdown tools.
Explain how to use diagnostic tools including cable testers, toner probes, and loopback plugs.
Summarize the function of Wi-Fi analyzers and network taps in diagnosing network connectivity.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.description: Recognize, install, and configure cables, displays, motherboards, storage, and printers.

topics.title: Display Components and Attributes
topics.description: Compare and contrast display components, technologies, and attributes.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between LCD (IPS, TN, VA), OLED, and Mini-LED display types.
Identify internal display components like touch screens, digitizers, and inverters.
Define monitor attributes such as pixel density, refresh rates, screen resolution, and color gamut.

topics.title: Network and Peripheral Cables
topics.description: Summarize the basic types, connectors, and purposes of network and peripheral cables.
learning_objectives: Identify copper networking cable characteristics including T568A/T568B, coaxial, UTP, and STP.
Differentiate between single-mode and multimode optical cables and plenum-rated jackets.
Compare peripheral connection cables like USB 2.0/3.0, Serial, and Thunderbolt.

topics.title: Video and Storage Cables
topics.description: Summarize the basic types, connectors, and purposes of video and hard drive cables.
learning_objectives: Distinguish between video cables such as HDMI, DisplayPort, DVI, and VGA.
Identify hard drive cables including SATA and eSATA.
Recognize specific connector types like RJ11, RJ45, F-type, ST, SC, LC, Molex, and Lightning.

topics.title: RAM Characteristics
topics.description: Compare and contrast Random Access Memory (RAM) characteristics.
learning_objectives: Identify RAM form factors including SODIMM and DIMM modules.
Explain Double Data Rate (DDR) iterations and the difference between ECC and non-ECC RAM.
Summarize the benefits of multi-channel memory configurations.

topics.title: Storage Devices
topics.description: Compare and contrast properties of hard drives, solid-state drives, and varied drive configurations.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between traditional hard drives (spindle speeds, sizes) and solid-state drives (NVMe, SATA).
Identify SSD form factors and interfaces including M.2, mSATA, PCIe, and SAS.
Explain RAID configurations (0, 1, 5, 6, 10) and identify removable and optical storage media.

topics.title: Motherboards and BIOS/UEFI
topics.description: Install and configure motherboards, understanding form factors and BIOS/UEFI settings.
learning_objectives: Compare motherboard form factors (ATX, microATX, ITX) and connector types (PCI, PCIe, M.2, Headers).
Configure BIOS/UEFI settings including boot options, USB permissions, and secure boot.
Explain hardware security concepts like TPM and Hardware Security Modules (HSM).

topics.title: CPUs, Expansion Cards, and Cooling
topics.description: Install and configure central processing units, add-on expansion cards, and proper cooling mechanisms.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between CPU socket types (AMD vs Intel) and architectures (x86/x64, ARM).
Identify common expansion cards such as sound cards, video cards, capture cards, and NICs.
Select and apply appropriate cooling solutions including fans, heat sinks, thermal paste, and liquid cooling.

topics.title: Power Supplies
topics.description: Select and install the appropriate power supply based on scenario requirements.
learning_objectives: Distinguish between input voltages (110-120 VAC vs. 220-240 VAC) and required output DC voltages.
Identify physical characteristics of power supplies including modular vs non-modular, and 20+4 pin connectors.
Determine power requirements through wattage ratings, redundant power supplies, and energy efficiency standards.

topics.title: Printer Deployment and Configuration
topics.description: Deploy and configure multifunction devices, printers, and associated print settings.
learning_objectives: Demonstrate how to properly unbox, set up, and select connectivity options for a printer.
Install appropriate printer drivers (PCL vs. Postscript) and configure sharing or print server capabilities.
Configure device settings such as duplex, orientation, tray, quality, and secured prints.

topics.title: Printer Maintenance
topics.description: Perform appropriate routine maintenance across various types of printers.
learning_objectives: Perform laser printer maintenance including replacing toner, applying maintenance kits, and calibration.
Maintain inkjet printers by cleaning printheads, replacing cartridges, and clearing jams.
Execute maintenance on thermal and impact printers, such as replacing thermal paper/ribbons and removing debris.

sections.title: Virtualization and Cloud Computing
sections.description: Understand foundational virtualization requirements and cloud computing models.

topics.title: Virtualization Concepts
topics.description: Explain the purpose, requirements, and implementations of desktop and server virtualization.
learning_objectives: Identify the purpose of virtual machines for sandboxing, test development, and application virtualization.
List the core requirements needed to support virtualization, including security, network, and storage considerations.
Differentiate between Type 1 and Type 2 hypervisors, containers, and Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI).

topics.title: Cloud Computing Concepts
topics.description: Summarize standard cloud computing deployment models and fundamental service characteristics.
learning_objectives: Compare common cloud models including Private, Public, Hybrid, and Community.
Differentiate between service models such as IaaS, SaaS, and PaaS.
Describe key cloud characteristics including shared vs dedicated resources, elasticity, metered utilization, and multitenancy.

sections.title: Hardware and Network Troubleshooting
sections.description: Apply troubleshooting steps to resolve motherboard, drive, video, mobile, network, and printer issues.

topics.title: Troubleshooting Motherboards, RAM, CPUs, and Power
topics.description: Diagnose and troubleshoot hardware faults related to motherboards, memory, processors, and power supplies.
learning_objectives: Identify common symptoms of hardware failure such as POST beeps, blank screens, and random shutdowns.
Diagnose physical component issues like burning smells, capacitor swelling, and overheating.
Troubleshoot sluggish performance, crash screens, and inaccurate system date/time.

topics.title: Troubleshooting Drive and RAID Issues
topics.description: Diagnose and troubleshoot common storage drive failures and RAID array issues.
learning_objectives: Recognize physical indicators of drive failure such as grinding noises, clicking, and LED status indicators.
Troubleshoot logical storage errors including bootable device not found, missing drives in OS, and data corruption.
Identify and resolve RAID failures, missing arrays, audible alarms, and S.M.A.R.T. errors.

topics.title: Troubleshooting Display and Projector Issues
topics.description: Identify and resolve problems affecting monitors, projectors, and video display output.
learning_objectives: Diagnose common display hardware issues like burnt-out bulbs, physical cabling faults, and incorrect input source.
Resolve image quality problems such as fuzzy image, incorrect color display, dim images, and distorted display.
Identify symptoms like display burn-in, dead pixels, and flashing screens.

topics.title: Troubleshooting Mobile Device Issues
topics.description: Troubleshoot common issues with mobile device hardware, power, connectivity, and software.
learning_objectives: Diagnose hardware damage including broken screens, physically damaged ports, liquid damage, and digitizer issues.
Troubleshoot power problems like poor battery health, swollen batteries, overheating, and improper charging.
Identify and resolve connectivity, performance, and malware issues on mobile platforms.

topics.title: Troubleshooting Network Issues
topics.description: Isolate and resolve typical wired and wireless networking problems.
learning_objectives: Troubleshoot connectivity issues including intermittent wireless, slow speeds, and limited connectivity.
Diagnose network performance problems such as high latency, jitter, and poor VoIP quality.
Identify the root causes of external interference, port flapping, and authentication failures.

topics.title: Troubleshooting Printer Issues
topics.description: Troubleshoot and fix common symptoms associated with printing operations and output quality.
learning_objectives: Diagnose print quality issues such as lines down pages, garbled print, faded prints, and speckling/echo images.
Resolve mechanical issues including paper jams, multipage misfeeds, grinding noises, and finishing/stapler jams.
Troubleshoot software and connectivity faults like frozen print queues, tray not recognized, and connectivity drops.
